generalities.text: Main THE ENCYCLOPEDIA OF PURE MATERIA MEDICA By TIMOTHY F. ALLEN, A.M., M.D. Presented by Médi-T LINARIA. Linaria Vulgaris, Mill. Natural order , Scrophulariaceæ. Common names , Butter and eggs, Toad-flax; (G.), Frauenflachs, Leinkraut. Preparation , Tincture of the plant. Authorities. 1 , Dr. Muller, Zeit. d. Ver. d. Hom. st., 1857, vol. 1, p. 41, effects of 10 drops of tincture; 2 , Jenicek A., same, vol. 2, p. 296, effects of repeated doses of 5 to 30 drops of tincture; 3 , Maria St., aged 32, took repeated doses, 5 to 8 drops, ibid.; 4 , Dr. Raidl, took 5 to 30 drops of tincture, ibid. MIND. ► Emotional. ► Peevish mood, [4] . ► Indifference (first day), [3] . ► Intellectual. ► General dulness (first day), [3] . ► Stupid, feeling of indifference, ill-humor, with dull pain in the forehead, with heat after the sleep, lasting till night, [2] . HEAD. ► Confusion of the head (after 10 drops), [4] . ► Confusion of the head, with irresistible sleepiness (first day), [3] . ► Head very much confused all day (after 20 drops), [4] . ► Pressure in the frontal region (after 20 drops), [4] . MOUTH. ► Dryness of the tongue, in the morning on waking, associated with some thirst, so that I desired to drink water before rising, which was very unusual; water, however, did not have a good taste, it seemed to have something rough in it, and I noticed that it glided unpleasantly over the tongue, and in fact, an examination, showed that the taste-papillæ were somewhat elevated, [1] . [10.] ► Tongue dry, with a prickling sensation along its margins and tip (after 20 drops), [4] . ► A burning prickling sensation on the tongue that extended down into the throat and caused a tickling irritation to cough, on smoking the first cigar in the morning; I was scarcely able to continue smoking, [1] . ► Increased secretion of saliva, which causes frequent spitting, [2] . ► Taste of blood in the mouth, [1] . THROAT. ► Feeling of constriction in the throat, [2] ; (second day), [3] . ► Sensation of constriction in the throat, as if a ball were lodged in it which should be swallowed, [3] . STOMACH. ► Appetite. ► Appetite diminished (first day), [3] ; (after 10 drops), [4] . ► Loss of appetite. ► Thirst. ► Thirst (after 20 drops), [4] . ► Eructation. ► Frequent eructations, [2] ; (first day), [3] . [20.] ► Frequent eructations preceding and following the usual stool (after one hour, first day), [3] . ► Empty eructations (after fifteen minutes), [4] . ► Eructations of odorless air; afterwards also of water, [3] . ► Eructations of odorless air, with some discomfort, nausea, and frequent involuntary shivering of the whole body (after half an hour), [2] . ► Sweetish eructations, [1] . ► Frequent bitter eructations (after 20 drops), [4] . ► Nausea and Vomiting. ► Nausea, [2] . ► General sick feeling, with sleepiness and sleep for two hours, between 10 and 12 in the forenoon, [2] . ► Inclination to vomit (first day), [3] . ► Vomiting of thin mucous substances, followed by disappearance of all the gastric symptoms (first day), [3] . ► Stomach. [30.] ► Pressure in the stomach, [3] . ► Pressure in the stomach, with frequent eructations, [2] . ► Pressure in the epigastric region (after 20 drops), [4] . ► Very acute pressure in the stomach, aggravated by touch, [2] . ABDOMEN. ► Griping about the umbilicus, which, from time to time, increases in intensity (after 20 drops), [4] . ► Rumbling in the intestines (first day), [3] . ► Rumbling in the abdomen, and in the course of two hours, four watery stools, with very little bile-pigment (after 20 drops), [4] . ► Flatulence causes an uneasy sensation in the abdomen, [1] . ► Griping in the abdomen, followed after half an hour by a watery stool (after 10 drops), [4] . ► Griping in the intestines, followed after an hour by the usual stool (after fifteen minutes), [4] . ANUS. [40.] ► Burning in the anus, [4] . STOOL. ► Diarrha; stools thin, four times (second day), [3] . ► Ten watery stools, containing very little bile-pigment, with emission of much flatus (after 30 drops), [4] . ► Seven watery stools of a very pale color, with emission of much flatus (after second dose of 30 drops), [4] . ► Four thin stools during the day, [2] . ► Three thin stools in one morning, [2] . ► Very dark-colored fæces, [1] . URINARY ORGANS. ► Great desire to urinate, with frequent micturition; urine increased in quantity and frothy, but unchanged in other respects (first night), [1] . ► Copious secretion of urine, [1] . ► Urine diminished in quantity, and of a dark color, [4] . [50.] ► Urine very high-colored, like dark beer, and passed more frequently than usual, [2] . SEXUAL ORGANS. ► Menstruation a few days earlier than usual, [3] . ► Menstruation which had just ceased returned the same day that she took a new dose, [3] . CHEST. ► Oppression of the chest that made respiration difficult, and caused cough; violent paroxysms of cough, so that at times I was obliged to stand quite still, in order the more easily to cough it out, and regain my breath, [1] . ► Transient stitches in the chest, [2] . ► Transient stitches, here and there, deep in the chest and abdomen (first day), [3] . GENERAL SYMPTOMS. ► General prostration, [4] . ► Symptoms were relieved for a short time after taking tea with milk (renewed quite severely and persistently on walking in the open air), [1] . SKIN. ► Itching on the throat, chest, and upper part of the back, [4] . SLEEP. ► Frequent yawning and stretching of the limbs, with a feeling of weakness and prostration (first day), [3] . [60.] ► Sleepiness, [4] . ► Irresistible sleepiness (first day), [3] . ► Sleep the forepart of the night quiet and refreshing, on waking felt invigorated, so that it seemed as though he had slept the whole night until late in the morning, and he was quite astonished to find that it was only half past twelve; the remainder of the night was sleepless, with confused dreams towards morning, [1] . FEVER. ► Coldness (first day), [3] . ► Alternations of chill and heat (first day), [2] . CONDITIONS. ► Aggravation. ► ( Morning ), On rising dryness of tongue, etc. ► ( Forenoon ), Between 10 and 12 o'clock, sick feeling. ► ( Walking in open air ), The symptoms, [1] . ► Amelioration. ► ( Tea with milk ), The symptoms, [1] . Clinical: Diarrhœa; Enuresis; Fainting; Hæmorrhoids; Ophthalmia; Tongue, roughness of; prickling on

Violent paroxysm of cough, obliged to stand quite still to cough it out and regain breath. General prostration. Symptoms > for short time after drinking tea with milk; severely and persistently on walking in open air.

keynotes.text: Linaria has been proved by Müller, Raidl, and others. It produced confusion in the head; thirst, with rough tongue; constriction of the throat; nausea, vomiting, and diarrhœa; profuse urination; oppression of the breathing and stitches in the chest. Farrington commends it for cardiac fainting: "faints dead away without apparent cause"; and also in enuresis: "enuresis with frequent painful urging to urinate causing patient to rise at night." Carleton (H. P., xii. 526) has seen it produce,: "fainting feeling three or four times a day; fainted away completely once." Coldness was a prominent feature, and the symptoms were much by walking in open air; > by taking tea with milk. In domestic practice Linar. has long been used as a lotion for inflamed eyes, and as an application for painful piles. It is said to be purgative and diuretic.

_raw: Antidoted by: tea in milk. Compare: Digit. (fainting), and other Scrophulariæ. Caust., Eu. purp., Equiset. (enuresis).

mental_emotional.text: Peevish mind. Indifference. Stupid feeling of indifference, ill-humour, with dull pain in forehead, with heat after the sleep, lasting till midnight.

head.text: Confusion of the head with irresistible sleepiness. Pressure in forehead.

mouth_throat.text: Dryness of tongue, morning on waking, with thirst for water before rising, water tasted badly, seemed rough, glided unpleasantly over tongue, papillæ elevated. Burning prickling on tongue, extending down into throat, smoking = tickling irritation to cough, could scarcely continue to smoke. Saliva increased, frequent spitting. Taste of blood in mouth. Sensation of constriction in throat; as if a ball lodged in it which should be swallowed.

stomach_abdomen.text: Appetite: diminished; lost. Thirst. Eructations: frequent, preceding and following usual stool; of odourless air, afterwards also of water; sweetish; bitter. General sick feeling with sleepiness and sleep for two hours (10 a.m. to 12). Vomiting of thin, mucous substances followed by disappearance of all gastric symptoms. Very acute pressure in stomach by touch. Griping about umbilicus, increasing in intensity from time to time. Rumbling, and in the course of two hours five watery stools with very little pigment. Griping, half an hour afterwards a watery stool.

stool_rectum.text: Burning in anus. Diarrhœa; stools thin; ten watery, bileless stools with much flatus. Very dark-coloured fæces.

urinary.text: Great desire; frequent inclination; quantity increased and frothy. Urine very high-coloured, like dark beer, passed more frequently. (Enuresis.).

female.text: Menses a few days early. Menses which had just ceased returned the same day she took a new dose.

chest_heart.text: Oppression, making respiration difficult and causing cough. Transient stitches here and there, deep in chest and abdomen.

skin.text: Itching in throat, chest, and upper back.

sleep.text: Frequent yawning and stretching with feeling of weakness and prostration. Irresistible sleepiness. Sleep forepart of night, quiet and refreshing as if he had slept all night; rest of night sleepless with confused dreams towards morning.

fever_chill.text: Coldness. Alternations of chills and heat. Copyright © Médi-T ® 2000 Main.

indications.text: The plant Linaria vulgaris, Miller (Antirrhinum Linaria, Linné). Nat. Ord.—Scrophulariaceae. COMMON NAMES: Snap-dragon, Butter and eggs, Toad flax, Ramsted.

Botanical Source.—This plant is a perennial whose stem reaches a height of from 1 to 2 feet, and has pale-green, smooth, lance-linear, crowded, sessile, alternate leaves. The flowers, yellow and orange in color, are dense and imbricate, and borne in a showy terminal spike or raceme. The corolla is personate and its base extended into a spur. The calyx is smooth and not so long as the curved spur.

History.—Linaria is a native of Europe, but is naturalized in this country, where it is common in waste places, and sometimes becomes so plentiful in fields as to become a nuisance. When fresh the plant has a nauseously unpleasant odor, which it loses for the most part upon drying. Its taste is subacrid, bitter, and slightly saline. It should be gathered when in bloom, which is in July and August, quickly and carefully dried, and placed in close containers protected from light and air. A yellow coloring substance (anthokirrin) was obtained by Rigel, in 1843, from the blossoms. Walz, in 1854, isolated antirrhinic acid (a peculiar volatile substance); linarosmin (an oily residue from the distillation with water); bitter crystalline linariin, an acrid resin linaracrin, and tannic and citric acids, gum, sugar, mineral matters, etc.

Action, Medical Uses, and Dosage.—This plant is recommended for "bad blood," splenic and hepatic hypertrophies, jaundice, skin diseases, and scrofula. An ointment prepared by covering 1 part of the bruised fresh plant with 10 parts of hot lard or mutton tallow, forms a soothing application for hemorrhoids and similar conditions. A strong tincture (℥viii to alcohol 98 per cent Oj), may be given in doses of a fraction of a drop to 10 drops. A decoction is prepared from 1 ounce of the plant to 1 pint of water.
King's American Dispensatory, 1898, was written by Harvey Wickes Felter, M.D., and John Uri Lloyd, Phr. M., Ph. D.
